reports.incident_description: Diet slip notes G6PD + seafood allergy for bed 12, but lunch tray arrived with dried sprats curry. Please recall and replace; update allergy flag on the diet ticket.
After the brief morning rain, two red rice sacks near the loading bay feel damp at the base. Request inspection, segregation, and moisture log entry before use.
Post-OGTT patient was given sweetened tea from the ward trolley. Kindly supply unsweetened tea and review diabetic beverage default for OPD holds.
Vegetarian dhal and fish curry labels were swapped during lunch rush; two staff complained. Need immediate relabel, announce correction, and reinforce color-code SOP.
Raw chicken tray stored above cut veg, drip risk noted. Quarantine affected veg and sanitize racks.
Patient on NBM except meds still received full breakfast (string hoppers + kiri hodi). Please pull back ENT trays and recheck the morning diet list printout.
Nepro vanilla is out of stock; three HD patients require evening feeds. Arrange alternative renal formula and update procurement ETA today.
Morning milk delivery recorded at 12°C due to sunny offload delay. Please decide on rejection or rapid chill with documented deviation.
Neutropenic diet trays had fresh coriander garnish. Kindly replace with plain, no raw herbs, and brief the cook on neutropenic restrictions.
After the brief evening rain, a drip started over the bakery display; pastry boxes became damp. Need immediate discard of affected items and cordon area.
Mother on vegetarian diet requested no onion/garlic per religious vow, but lunch list shows standard veg curry. Please issue a ‘satvik’ variant from today.
Halal certification signage not displayed at the chicken counter and utensils were mixed. Require dedicated ladles, signage, and service staff briefing.
Hot staff lunch packs were placed on the formula prep bench. Request immediate deep clean and reminder to keep NICU milk area food-free.
Tuna tins expiring next week found at back of shelf, not FIFO. Please adjust menu to rotate stock or arrange return before lapse.
Low-salt breakfast arrived with regular parippu; two hypertensive patients flagged. Kindly recall the batch and send renal/low-sodium replacements.
Walk-in chiller temp rose to 9°C due to frequent door openings; raw fish trays affected. Need discard decision and temp recovery plan.
High clinic crowd; vegetarian counter sold out by 12:05 PM. Request rapid batch of red rice + pumpkin curry and adjust forecast for Tuesdays.
Child on vegetarian diet was served egg curry. Please correct diet card coding and send veg protein alternative (soya or dhal).
NBM patient given a cup of plain tea from the snack trolley while waiting for theatre. Request route change for trolley and bold ‘NBM Zone’ signage.
Dysphagia orders for Ward 6 require IDDSI Level 2, but lunch jellies arrived Level 3. Please reissue fluids at correct thickness and update prep guide.
Morning mist led to condensation; gotukola bundles arrived wilted and slimy at the cores. Need partial rejection and switch to mallung with cabbage.
Wet ramp from afternoon rain caused soup urn to slip during Ward 3 delivery; minor spill. Request anti-slip mats on trolley route and replace spilled portions.
Turmeric and chilli powder in open bins clumped with a slight musty smell. Please quality-check, discard if needed, and seal containers.
Salad bar had cut papaya left out beyond 2 hours during warm lunch. Please discard exposed trays and reinforce time/temperature controls.
Coconut milk stock is short for lunch service; several patients have nut allergies recorded. Request menu switch to dhal-based gravy and confirm allergen-safe prep.
Paediatric bed 12 (lactose-intolerant) did not receive the prescribed lactose-free formula with breakfast; only regular milk is on the ward. Please dispatch correct feed and update the tray list.
Afternoon snack bread packets arrived with damp outer wrapping after brief rain; request replacement and review transport covers for patient snacks.
At the staff cafeteria, Halal-certified items were not clearly labelled or segregated. Please institute proper signage and service line separation.
Two CKD patients on renal low-sodium/low-potassium diets received regular parippu and a banana; trays appear swapped. Need immediate correction and review of diet tickets.
Only 12 tins of renal formula remain; average use is 10 per day. Please raise urgent LPO and check pending deliveries.
Diet tickets requested puree (IDDSI 4) but minced and moist (IDDSI 5) was delivered for three patients. Risk of aspiration if not corrected.
Blast chiller logged 8-9 C for cut fruit during lunch prep; today is very humid and doors were opened frequently. Hold service for those items and apply corrective cooling.
Two 25 kg flour sacks show moisture staining at the base. Quarantined for QA check and supplier advice.
Rice and curry ran out by 12:45 PM; only string hoppers remained at the counter. Please review lunch production forecast and staff meal counts.
Near-miss: Tray for Ward 5 included sprats fry though the diet card flags fish allergy. Item removed before service, but utensil and line segregation need reinforcement.
Morning milk chiller reading 9 C; door gasket is torn. All dairy held for temperature check and disposition; request urgent repair.
Due to the heat, wards requested extra lime juice and ORS; stock of ORS sachets and limes is low. Please approve supplemental purchase today.
A drip was noted over dry store shelf C; sugar and salt packets are clumping. Stock shifted; need dehumidifier and rack inspection.
Supplier delay due to rain led to last-minute poultry substitution for planned seer fish. Renal ward menu was not updated; please coordinate substitutions with wards.
Wet floor near the kitchen back door caused a trolley skid; 14 covered trays toppled and may be contaminated. Require immediate remake and delivery delay notice.
NPO patient (Ward 7, first on theatre list) received a breakfast tray due to an outdated diet census. Pull back urgently and correct dispatch lists.
Cardiology tally requested 8 low-salt meals but 12 were delivered; 4 returned unopened. Please audit ticketing to prevent waste.
Dishwasher final rinse logged 70-72 C during dinner wash; target is 82 C. Implement manual sanitization and arrange immediate repair.
Morning drizzle leaked through the loading bay awning; one 50 kg samba rice bag is wet at the base. Bag isolated; request supplier guidance and cover repair.
Patient requested vegetarian with no onion/garlic; tray contained leeks tempered. Please adjust recipe and tag the special diet clearly.
OPD nutrition corner received yoghurt cups without date/time labels and no cold-chain log on the tote. Relabelling and transport SOP refresher needed.
Diet tickets use LS/NS codes in small font; near mix-up reported on Ward 9. Request higher-contrast print and colour bands on tickets.
Overnight power fluctuation recorded; walk-in freezer peaked at -5 C for 50 minutes. Need product safety assessment and discard decision per policy.
Evening gusts caused exhaust backdraft; dust settled on the hopper station. Discarded exposed items; please review canopy and air curtain settings.
Diabetic patient in Ward 18 received regular jelly at breakfast; sugar-free jelly not available on the tray line. Replace and check dessert stock for diabetic diets.
Walk-in chiller reading 7.6°C since 09:40; high ambient humidity this morning. Perishable milk and yogurt inside need temp recovery plan.
Water seeped under the dry-store door; bases of three rice sacks are wet. Request quarantine and supplier claim.
Lunch trays reached 35 minutes late due to rain; two diabetic patients received regular rice from ward fallback. Need immediate replacement with diabetic portions.
Chicken short-eats at staff canteen lacked visible Halal certification; Muslim staff objected. Need vendor verification and signage.
Low-salt diet soup tasted noticeably salty; BP-restricted patients returned portions. Please check recipe and batch salinity.
Low-potassium formula sachets stockout; only two packets left for Ward 9B. Urgent substitution protocol or emergency procurement.
Brief power dip blast chiller stopped mid-cycle for cooked fish. Core temps remained above 10°C for 25 min; HACCP corrective action needed.
Flies near waste hatch by dishwash area; current fly screens not sealing. Risk to hygiene around plating line.
On Poya day, a vegetarian-designated patient was served fish curry. Please ensure cultural diet flags print on tray cards.
Patient listed NPO from midnight was sent a tray with kiri bath and tea at 06:00. Diet order on system shows 'fasting'; request stop-gap check before breakfast dispatch.
Tea and coffee tasted earthy after last night’s rain; kitchen filter candles look due for change. Please switch to boiled-filtered service till resolved.
Tray for child with documented peanut allergy included unlabeled pol sambol prepared near peanut sambol. Intercepted before serving; need strict segregation and labels.
Dysphagia thickener tins (lot expiring 20/03) still in main store; wards may not use in time. Request FIFO enforcement and reallocation to high-use wards.
Newly-admitted diabetic patient got sweetened tea with breakfast. Please ensure 'no-sugar beverages' default for DM flags at admission.
Due to midday rain, fish supplier delayed; kitchen substituted vegetable curry in renal lunches without notifying diet office, altering protein targets.
Condensation drip observed from evaporator in walk-in chiller after storm; two marinated chicken trays were uncovered beneath. Potential contamination—need discard and maintenance coordination.
Staff canteen ran out of rice and parippu by 01:00 PM; service contract requires supply till 02:30 PM. Forecasting and batch scheduling need correction.
Gusty wind with storm blew dust through vegetable prep louvres; coriander and chopped greens contaminated. Batch should be discarded; request screens or temporary covers.
Grab-and-go yoghurt cups found at 27–28°C during spot check; chiller overloaded in afternoon heat. Immediate disposal and load limit needed.
Enteral feed bag labeled 'standard' delivered to CICU; order is 'renal' low-electrolyte. Error detected before hang—need double-verification at formula room.
Afternoon burst of rain soaked bread crates during offloading; several loaves damp. Request return to supplier and covered receiving area usage.
Cafeteria display chiller holding cut fruit at 11–12°C in humid conditions; condenser clogged. Remove items and service unit before reopening.
Low-sodium soups vary widely in taste across days; patients either refuse or complain blandness. Please standardize recipe and document grams of salt per batch.
Service corridor wet; breakfast trolleys rerouted and arrived 20–25 min late to Maternity. Need contingency timing and heated cart check.
Power outage pushed cold-room temp to 8.4°C for ~20 min; raw fish and dairy affected. Initiate discard per policy and document product loss.
Breakfast curd cups reached the ward warm in this morning’s heat; cold trolley packs didn’t hold temperature. Request review of cold-chain for early trays.
Multiple flies observed at the OPD cafeteria salad bar; sneeze guard not fully closed. Immediate sanitation and service hold recommended.
Evening thunderstorm caused a brief power dip; deli chiller rose to 12°C for ~25 min with sliced chicken inside. Need discard decision and temperature log review.
Two 50 kg rice sacks got wet during the afternoon downpour near loading bay; stitching damp and slight odor noted. Request quarantine and supplier claim.
Patient on clear-liquid diet received thick parippu soup with solids. Please correct therapeutic diet and audit tray ticketing.
Post-storm roof drip over vegetable prep bench; sanitize area and discard cut salad prepared during the leak.
Suhur tray for fasting Muslim patient not delivered; Halal items and timing list not followed. Please activate Ramadan/fasting schedule.
Low stock of renal-specific oral supplement (vanilla) in unit pantry; only one tin left for today’s HD patients. Request urgent replenishment.
Peanut sambol spoon used to serve vegetarian tray for a patient with peanut allergy; caught before serving. Need strict utensil segregation and allergen flagging.
Lunch biryani on hotline measured 50–54°C; hot cabinet not holding due to ambient heat. Urgent corrective action required.
Eight tins of fibre-enriched enteral formula in the dietary store have past expiry dates. Please quarantine and adjust stock rotation (FEFO).
Kokis for staff event fried in same oil as fish cutlets; allergen cross-contact not labelled at the cafeteria counter.
Dishwasher final rinse not reaching sanitizing temperature after today’s rain-induced low water pressure. Hold patient tray crockery until verified.
Warfarin patient served gotukola mallum with dinner; high vitamin K conflicts with anticoagulation. Need renal/cardiac/anticoagulant diet guardrails revised.
High humidity causing rapid banana over-ripening; ~6 kg likely wastage. Request dehumidifier use or adjusted ripening/issue schedule.
Patient on Level 4 pureed diet received grainy dhal and string hopper mash; unsafe for dysphagia. Please review texture standards and blender maintenance.
Tea and short eats for CME session arrived 30 minutes late after the thunderstorm; items were soggy. Need contingency for wet-weather catering.
Generator switchover during lightning caused walk-in chiller to hit 10°C for ~45 min with raw poultry inside. Require immediate product safety decision and incident CAPA.
Buddhist monk patient received chicken broth jelly instead of vegetarian clear soup. Please enforce religious preference flagging on the diet card.
Child with lactose intolerance was served regular milk tea at breakfast instead of lactose-free. Please correct beverage list and ward pantry labels.
After today’s rain, flour and sugar bins near the dry store door show clumping; weevils seen in one atta bin. Request pest control and batch disposal as needed.
Cafeteria salad bar lacks allergen labels for cashew and sesame items. Immediate compliant labelling and staff briefing required.
Ice machine output very low in the afternoon heat; cold fluids for febrile patients delayed. Please arrange supplemental ice and service check.
Diabetic trays are delaed by 45 minutes; insulin-timed meals missed. Need delivery reroute plan for heavy rain days.
Tins of high-protein supplement received dented with incomplete batch trace on GRN. Please hold stock and coordinate with Procurement for replacement.
Diabetic breakfast tray arrived with sweetened milk tea and banana for patient on 1600 kcal diabetic diet. Please replace and correct the diet card.
During heavy downpour, water seeped into dry store; bases of two 25 kg rice sacks are damp. Request inspection, segregation, and rack placement.
Bain-marie holding chicken curry dropped to 55°C with drafts from rain-soaked entrance. Need reheat or hold from sale and temperature log update.
Tube-feed fridge reading 12°C after night storm. Yogurt and renal supplements stored inside; request discard per SOP and urgent replacements.
Vendor delayed by local flooding; leafy greens not delivered. Please confirm menu substitutions for lunch/dinner and notify wards.
Low-sodium dinner included dried-fish mallung. Patient on 2 g Na diet; request immediate replacement and recipe salt review.
Squally winds blew dust into loading bay; open bread crates were exposed during unloading. Need product replacement and area sanitizing.
Cafeteria biriyani not labeled halal today; supplier certificate not displayed. Please verify status, label correctly, or remove from service.
Child with lactose intolerance received normal milk at tea. Already sipped. Replace with lactose-free and flag allergy on tray card.
Breakfast arrived as soft solids instead of pureed (IDDSI 4). Aspiration risk. Please resend correct texture and check line controls.
Juice cooler off ~30 min, now 9°C. Packed juices and cut fruit inside; need hold/discard decision and log entry.
Hair strand reported in vegetarian curry to Ward 7A. Request batch trace, staff hygiene check, and immediate tray replacements.
Renal breakfast tray included papaya for haemodialysis patient. Please adjust renal menu and update ward restriction list.
Condenser icing; cold room temp fluctuated 4–10°C. Need immediate product temperature checks and transfer of high-risk items.
Liquidized diets too thin; new thickener not gelling as per spec. Patients on NGT at risk of low intake. Please QA and issue alternate brand.
Pre-op carbohydrate drinks for 08:00 OT list not delivered. Storms earlier may have delayed. Require urgent dispatch to OT reception.
Leak near veg prep boards and prepped veg splashed. Recommend discard of affected items and full sanitization.
Patient identifies as strict vegetarian (Buddhist) but received fish curry at lunch. Please replace meal and correct preference log.
Received 30 tins Ensure vanilla expiring next month. Risk of wastage with current census. Request approval to return or reallocate to clinics.
Two diabetic trays arrived without carb counts on labels; insulin dosing delayed. Please resend labels and update diet sheet.
Rain increased crowd; short eats ran out. Staff queueing into corridor. Request catering backup or alternate hot item release.
Diet prep area had no paper towels; staff seen drying hands on aprons during rush. Immediate restock and food-handler retraining needed.
Breakfast kiri bath served with lunu miris; mother on reflux-friendly bland diet post-delivery. Please adjust and update ward list.
Fresh chicken delivery probed at 9°C after prolonged rain transit. Request reject/rapid-cook decision as per HACCP and supplier notification.
Four cartons of Nepro found burst; outer cartons water-damaged from rain. Please quarantine lot and arrange urgent replacement for renal wards.
Breakfast trays arrived 40 minutes late after morning downpour; insulin timing affected for two kids.
Bottom rice sacks damp; need inspection and segregation.
Walk-in chiller at 9°C after humid spell; cooked fish trays placed for cooling; initiate corrective action.
Plating area floor wet after thunderstorm; staff moving without shoe covers; urgent clean and SOP reminder.
No commercial thickener for breakfast; dysphagia patients pending safe fluids.
Renal diet included soup and papadam; exceeds sodium/fluid limits ordered.
Bain-marie showed 52°C post-lightning; ward curries lukewarm.
Fish-allergic patient received katta sambol with Maldive fish (umbalakada); swapped late.
Strong wind along link corridor blew off soup lids on diet trolley; trays possibly contaminated.
Tea for diabetic ICU patients served with sugar despite 'no sugar' tags.
Cockroaches observed near dishwash drain after rain; request deep clean and sealing.
Cafeteria salad bar has peanuts in mallung but no allergen label.
Curd cups at OPD counter warm; one foil bulging; cold chain likely broken.
Post-op NPO patient received evening tray; update not reflected to kitchen.
Bread delivery cartons soaked in morning squall; request replacements and covered offloading.
Diet trolley wraps wet; ink on special-diet labels smeared; risk of misdelivery.
Drain backflow near veg prep after downpour; foul water close to food areas.
LPG regulator leaking on Line 2; meal production slowed; need spare now.
Lunch packs for clinic patients 25 minutes late due to rain; please adjust dispatching.
Tamil vegetarian patient served egg; diet card only in Sinhala; need corrected labels/translation.
PEG feed blenderisation coarse; equipment blade dull; choking risk for Level-4 diets.
Ceiling drip over cold room shelf; chickpea and milk powder cartons damp; quarantine stock.
Cafeteria foyer wet from rain; no mats/signage; near-slip observed.
No hot-holding temperature logs for staff cafeteria lunch service today.
Low-salt tray came with katta sambol and pickle; too salty for hypertensive patient.
Walk-in chiller peaked at 9°C for ~25 minutes during pre-dawn loading; door sweating in high humidity. Need product assessment and discard of high-risk items.
Egg and yoghurt cartons wet at loading bay. Request segregation, inspection, and receiving SOP adjustment for wet weather.
Low-potassium lunch trays were 20 minutes late; dialysis schedule affected. Need hot-box backup and staggered delivery plan during storms.
Diabetic tray had regular sago pudding with sugar syrup. Please review labelling controls for therapeutic diets on the tray line.
Ant trail observed near jaggery/sugar bins in dry store; lids not fully sealed. Request immediate deep clean and pest-control follow-up.
Dish machine final rinse logging 70–72°C; cannot assure sanitization for supper trays. Activate manual sanitizing protocol and call maintenance.
Cafeteria chicken curry at lunch measured 48°C on bain-marie. Reheat done; please document corrective action and verify holding temps.
Tray for child with severe peanut allergy included pol sambol from grinder used earlier for peanuts. Intercepted; enforce allergen controls and utensil color-coding.
Power flicker; ward nourishment fridge reset to 15°C. Opened enteral feeds for two patients warmed. Need replacements and discard guidance.
Rice ran out by 6 PM; supplier delayed due to flooded access road. Request buffer stock policy for monsoon days.
Halal certification signage missing at cafeteria during menu change. Muslim staff requesting source confirmation for chicken curry; please restore labels.
Dysphagia Level 2 patient (Ward 7) received dhal curry and tea at nectar-thick instead of honey-thick. Risk of aspiration; retrain on IDDSI levels.
Low-sodium diet patient received ambul thiyal (salted fish). Wrong diet ticket attached; investigate tray-line verification.
Nepro sachets out of stock; kitchen substituted standard milk powder for dialysis snack without approval. Urgent procurement and substitution policy needed.
Walk-in freezer heavy frost; temp at -10°C after wet deliveries with doors open. Request defrost plan and product temperature checks.
Lunch cart slipped on wet ramp; two trays opened and mixed. Meals replaced late. Need non-slip mats and covered transfer during rain.
Leak over Dry Store A dripping onto samba rice sacks; bases damp. Quarantined stock; request replacement and engineering follow-up.
Cafeteria queue backed into OPD triage. Please add extra cashier and pre-plated rice packs for peak wet hours.
Evening snack tea served sweetened to two diabetic clinic patients. Diet code D not reflected on beverage sheet; update production list.
Found six yoghurt cups expired yesterday in ward pantry supply. FIFO not followed. Request replacement and audit of rotation.
Parippu vadai batch undercooked (core 60°C) during lunch rush. Held and re-fired. Please check fryer calibration and record corrective action.
After rain, crates from dock arrived muddy; veg wash area overloaded. Need extra clean crates and boot-wash to prevent soil entering prep room.
Brown biofilm noted on cafeteria ice machine chute; filter change overdue. Halt ice for patient beverages; request deep clean and filter replacement.
Patient observing Poya requested vegetarian; soup sent was chicken-stock based. Please flag religious vegetarian requests on tickets clearly.
Main puree blender motor failed; cannot prepare IDDSI Level 4 breakfast textures. Need backup unit and urgent service; mashing is not adequate.
Breakfast tray for Bed 12 (CKD) included banana and dhal with added salt despite 2 g sodium, low-potassium order placed last night; please replace and review menu card update workflow.
Walk‑in dairy chiller reading rose to 9°C; pasteurised milk and curd inside—need immediate temperature recovery and product assessment.
Diabetic day-case meals arrived with sweetened kiri tea packets; request unsweetened replacements and check default beverage setting on OPD trays.
Require batch inspection and likely rejection as per hygiene SOP.
Cafeteria salad bar well is holding at 12°C during lunch rush; cold chain breach—need ice replenishment and unit service.
Heavy rain caused seepage along Store A wall; two rice sacks and one flour bag damp at base—quarantine stock and assess racks.
Supper trays reached Ward 6 only at 6:40 PM due to service lift outage; please arrange alternate delivery route and communicate revised timing.
Halal certificate not displayed at chicken counter today; Muslim staff asked for assurance—please place signage and segregate utensils.
Low‑sodium lunch soup on Beds 3 and 5 tasted salty; suspect standard stock used—kindly resend compliant portions and audit recipe card.
Polymeric enteral formula (vanilla) expires in 72 hours; ICU needs 12 units for weekend, store shows 10—urgent top‑up or approved substitute required.
Ants seen in parippu at breakfast line; trays pulled—request immediate halt, source check, and safe replacement.
Condensation from hood dripping onto hot holding line during rainy spell; risk of contamination—need drip trays and service.
Vegetarian renal patient (Bed 7) received paneer curry with tomato gravy not aligned to low‑potassium plan; please substitute and flag on diet sheet.
Walk‑in chiller door gasket warped; temp trending 7°C by noon in today’s heat—request urgent gasket replacement and interim loading limits.
Gusty rain washed food‑waste liquids from overflowed bin across cafeteria service alley; odour and vector risk—please sanitise area and adjust waste schedule.
Patient on puree Level 2 dysphagia diet received normal rice and fish at dinner; resend correct texture and check ticketing on theatre step‑down beds.
ORS bottles sold out at OPD kiosk before clinic peak; request restock and adjust forecast for Tuesday diabetic/HTN clinic crowd.
Celiac child (Bed 3) got chapati on lunch tray; gluten‑free order on chart—please replace and reinforce GF segregation.
Nut‑free snack pack issued to Ward 7B contained biscuit with groundnut trace warning; severe peanut allergy noted—immediate replacement and supplier review needed.
Thunderstorm caused power dip; blast chiller failed to pull chicken curry below 5°C—batch at 18°C after 90 min; initiate discard protocol.
Spinach crates wet—need drainage check and evaluate spoilage.
Halal label missing on patient dinner tray; family refused service—please resend with certification and update meal ticket.
Two Male Surgical beds with same surname got swapped diets; renal tray went to normal diet patient—request corrected meals and ID check at dispatch.
Yoghurt cups arrived to NICU pantry warm to touch (no ice packs in carrier); please replace and enforce cold‑chain for special care units.
Cafeteria pol sambol very salty and spicy during HTN clinic; please provide low‑salt option on clinic days and display sodium info.
Breakfast tray for a diabetic patient arrived with a sugar packet and sweetened milk tea instead of diet tea. Please replace and review tray checks.
Vegetable delivery arrived damp after brief rain; pre-cut pumpkin packs probed at 11–12°C. Need decision to accept or reject and adjust supplier log.
During evening rain, a roof drip developed above the hot line; water splashing near the parippu well. Risk of contamination and slips.
Renal (low K) patient sent a banana with lunch. Kindly replace and reinforce renal restrictions with plating team.
Rainwater tracked into the loading bay made the cold room entry slick; no floor mats. Staff nearly slipped while shifting milk crates.
Raw fish cartons stored above cooked dhal trays due to rain delay; observed drip marks on shelf. Immediate segregation and discard needed.
Child with severe egg allergy received custard from canteen via voucher; no allergen label on display. Please fix labelling and stop sale to paeds visitors until corrected.
Power dip, walk-in freezer rose to −7°C; door seal sweating. Need product temp checks, rapid recovery plan, and log entry.
Twelve liquid diet cups in ward pantry not labeled with patient names/time of preparation. Risk of mix-up for post-op clears.
Carbohydrate counts on diabetic lunch trays are off; rice portions appear larger than the diet sheet. Please standardize scoops.
Drinks chiller reading 12°C in hot afternoon; bottled yoghurt feels warm. Request to pull stock and check equipment.
Patient on pureed diet received soft solids (string hoppers with coconut sambol). Choking risk—send correct texture and review ticketing.
Ordered rice stock still at the store . please clear it out
NPO patient for afternoon list was served tea and biscuits from the morning trolley. Please update NPO hold list and stop meals to that bed.
Low-sodium bread is out of stock; wards are substituting regular bread. Need urgent supply or approved alternative.
Cafeteria ceiling leak dripping over the salad bar during thundery rain. Shut the section and discard exposed items.
Supplier truck stuck due to waterlogging; night supper chicken may run short. Please approve menu substitution and notify wards.
Muslim patient reported no visible Halal certification on the beef curry used for therapeutic tray. Kindly verify batch and replace.
Brief power cut lowered hot cabinet to 40–45°C for ~25 minutes; samosas/cutlets in danger zone. Need discard decision and reheating protocol.
Renal-specific enteral formula stock finished; only standard formula on hand. Request emergency issue or dietitian-approved substitution.
Back dock water pooling with heavy rain; vegetable crates placed on floor got splashed. Need raised pallets and crate sanitizing.
Cafeteria chocolate muffins lack peanut allergen warning; paeds carers often purchase them. Please label and restrict sale near ward.
Patient on lactose-free diet was served normal milk tea at supper. Kindly resend appropriate beverage and fix diet tag.
With widespread rain, drain backflow splashed near vegetable prep sink. Area requires deep sanitization and discard of prepped greens.
Incoming chicken mince cartons lack Halal stamp documentation; planned for staff curry. Hold or return and adjust menu.
Diabetic soft breakfast tray arrived with two sugar sachets and sweet banana; please correct diet packing and remove added sugars.
Evening thunderstorm caused a power dip; walk-in chiller hit 10°C for ~20 minutes—need decision on discarding pre-portioned chicken for late trays.
Renal (low potassium) patient was served jackfruit curry from the standard veg set; request immediate replacement and review of renal tagging.
Dry store feels damp after morning drizzle; two flour sacks near the wall show moisture stains—please quarantine and inspect for spoilage.
Supplier delayed by flooded Galle Road; low-sodium bread will stock out before dinner unless alternate bakery engaged.
Child with documented nut allergy received vanilla custard topped with cashew—urgent replacement and kitchen allergen control review needed.
Order changed to fiber-free jejunal feed at 02:00; 05:30 tray still had standard fiber formula—please send correct feed immediately.
Drips falling into gravy pans—section should be closed until safe.
Dishwasher final rinse not reaching 82°C; after lightning the heater breaker may have tripped—sanitization compromised.
Two vegetarian patients received fish cutlets on lunch trays; request pure veg replacements and verification of diet codes.
Patient marked NPO for DKA management was sent a full rice tray at 6 pm; urgent halt on further meals to this patient.
Rainwater at loading bay; several 25 kg rice sacks stored on floor got wet at the base—need rebagging or disposal per SOP.
Post-op patient on clear liquids received thick dhal soup; please send clear broth/jeevani promptly.
ICU tube-feed stock (peptide formula) below par; only four tins left—request emergency issue or supplier contact before night feeds.
After power cut, blast chiller at 12°C; batch-cooked fish not cooled within 2 hours—seek authorization to discard and log incident.
Neutropenic diet tray included raw salad; please replace with fully cooked veg and review menu controls.
Halal requirement documented for two OPD boarders; breakfast beef curry served without halal label—send certified alternative.
Dinner trays delayed to Wards 5–7—need extra insulated carriers and revised delivery route.
Ward 10 pantry has yogurt cups expired on 02 Oct; request immediate removal and stock rotation training for ward pantries.
POS outage during squall led to long queue; hot hold times for string hoppers and curries exceeded—advise on discards and service recovery.
Sodium-restricted, thickened fluids ordered; dinner tray arrived without thickener sachets—patient at aspiration risk.
Pureed diet tray had solid papaya cubes; please resend correctly pureed meal for dysphagia patient.
Cafeteria service line floor slippery from tracked rain; no wet-floor signage at tray return—request immediate controls from food service area team.
Fresh milk delivered at 12°C after street flooding delays; recommend rejecting consignment and adjusting evening tea service.
Pantry fridges alarmed; paediatric ONS bottles now at 11°C—need replacement stock and guidance on disposal.
Walk-in chiller spiked to 9°C after multiple power flickers during the storm; raw chicken and fish inside. Need immediate temp recovery and decision on discard per HACCP.
Rainwater seeped under loading-bay shutter; bottom layer of samba rice sacks damp. Please isolate, check for mould, and improve palletisation.
Breakfast tray for BHT 0423 (CKD-V) came with bananas and kiri pani; patient is on strict potassium restriction. Request correct renal tray and review coding.
Flooded road delayed soft-diet lunch; arrived lukewarm (<50°C). Please resend in insulated carriers ASAP.
Leak over cafeteria bain-marie during intense rain; drips near hot line. Service should pause and contaminated items be replaced.
Marine fish received late in steady rain; temp 12–14°C with off-odour. Reject batch and confirm alternate protein for dinner.
Main kitchen blender overheated and tripped mid-breakfast; pureed textures for Stroke Unit pending. Need backup unit and service call.
OPD diabetic patient was served sweet milk tea from trolley. Please provide sugar-free option and clearer flask labels.
LPG bank-2 nearly empty; cylinder truck delayed in squally rain. Risk to lunch production—initiate rationing and priority cooking plan.
Halal verification label missing on roast chicken trays for Muslim patients. Ward is holding service until documentation or swap provided.
Post rain condensation above pastry prep; droplets forming over ready-to-eat items. Discard exposed food and reduce humidity.
Tea boiler gave a mild shock walls/floor damp after continuous rain. Please isolate unit immediately and arrange repair.
Diet slip updated to NPO/TPN for BHT 0115, but full liquid breakfast sent. Kindly halt tray and amend ICU diet list urgently.
Severe peanut allergy patient received chickpea sambol made in mixer previously used for peanut paste. Cross-contact risk—need safe replacement and allergen controls.
Lactose-free milk out of stock; three GI cases pending feeds. Please source immediate substitute and inform ward.
Breakfast trays included boiled egg for mothers flagged egg-free due to hyperemesis. Kindly resend with kandha and update list.
Receiving area floor wet from rain tracked in; veg crates set on pooled water. Need pallets, mopping, and produce sanitisation.
Neutropenic diet breach: cold dessert with fresh garnish sent to BHT 0211. Replace with sealed pasteurised option and review list.
Power cut during lunch; hot-holding stayed at 48–52°C for ~50 minutes. Policy requires discard and re-prep of affected trays.
Rodent droppings found behind parippu sacks after flooding. Quarantine compromised lots and arrange urgent pest control and deep clean.
Fish curry splashed onto two vegetarian trays during plating; both already sent to Ward 6. Need immediate replacements and splash guard.
Walk-in freezer has heavy frost; door not sealing and ice on floor causing slips. Request defrost, gasket repair, and area cordon.
Rice-cooker steam venting onto cut-fruit counter; condensate dripping onto papaya. Need to relocate fruit and separate hot line.
Delivery of preterm formula and UHT milk missed due to flooded road; NICU fortification stock will run out tonight. Request emergency borrowing or fast-track purchase.
Renal formula tins (18) expiring 03 Dec; wards not informed. Please redistribute to Nephrology/ICU immediately and adjust ordering.
Afternoon thunderstorm caused gutter overflow; seepage into dry store made two bottom rice sacks damp. Request disposal assessment and check roof/drain near dry goods.
Lunch trays arrived 25 minutes late after rain left OPD corridor slippery and trolley had to detour. Need an extra insulated trolley and non-slip mats during wet weather.
Cafeteria ran out of curd and fruit by 12:20 despite clear weather; par levels underestimated for staff rush. Please adjust forecast and reorder.
Salad chiller logged 11°C after a humid, rainy morning; condensate noted on shelves. All ready-to-eat salads quarantined pending temperature recovery and QA check.
Renal diet patient received dhal with added salt and tomato (not K+ restricted). Tray returned; request immediate review of diet ticket and cook’s sheet.
Ant trail observed inside sugar bin; lid not sealed after morning tea round. Request pest control notification and replacement of contaminated sugar.
Puree station blender jar cracked during prep; minced/pureed diets due at 11:30. Need spare jar issued and damaged unit removed.
Hot meal trolley skidded and two rice trays spilled near theatre. Require immediate resupply and area sanitisation by dietary staff.
Cafeteria chicken curry not clearly marked Halal/non-Halal at lunch. Please add signage and separate ladles to maintain compliance.
Leafy greens wilting in vegetable room; humidity felt high after early light rain. Request rotation and exhaust fan check to protect salad quality.
Renal-specific enteral formula down to 12 tins with two ICU patients dependent; supplier lead time reportedly 3 days. Please expedite procurement and arrange interim stock.
Thunderstorm tripped bain-marie; gravies on the line measured 52–55°C. Hold service for those items and reheat per SOP.
Leak over salad bar during heavy rain; droplets on sneeze guard. Discard exposed dishes and cover station until safe; request urgent clean-up and liaison for repair.
Low-salt dhal and regular dhal labels swapped on the tray line. Caught prior to serving; need relabel and quick huddle with serving staff.
LPG pressure dropped and chapati line slowed; dinner to wards delayed 20 minutes. Request spare cylinder staging near hot line.
Vegetarian patient received fish curry despite preference noted. Please update ward list and segregate ladles to prevent cross-service.
Afternoon rain blew into loading bay; milk powder cartons became damp at base. Need integrity check and move to raised pallets.
Crushed peanuts prepped beside coconut sambol; same tray briefly used for renal meals. High allergen cross-contact risk—discard affected items and deep-clean area.
Chest freezer door not sealing; heavy frost and temp at −10°C. Frozen fish at risk—relocate stock and schedule immediate defrost/repair.
Fresh fish delivery arrived at 9°C; probe verified. Rejecting consignment and requesting menu switch to lentil/egg protein.
Short-eats and buns sold out by 11:30 due to holiday rush; queue extended into corridor. Need extra batch and second cashier to manage flow.
10:00 PM diabetic snack round missed; two insulin patients flagged risk. Request night snack checklist and sign-off from dietary porter.
Celiac patient in Ward 12 was given wheat bread instead of gluten-free; nurse intercepted before intake. Urgent segregation and red alert on diet tickets.
Festive lunch crowd exhausted rice by 1:00 PM; staff queue outside canteen. Please release backup rice cooker and tighten portion control.
Humid afternoon and large delivery caused walk-in chiller to hold 8–9°C for 35 minutes. Hold high-risk items and review loading/SOP to speed temp recovery.
